title: Carved wooden geometric panel
description: Rectangular carved wooden panel with repeating geometric cutout design.
significance: Represents decorative object holdings that may relate to African or African diasporic visual design traditions.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (38)/1.png. Title is provisional pending curator review.

title: Portrait photograph of two men
description: Black-and-white portrait-style image showing two men standing indoors.
significance: May document community leadership, family, or school-related history connected to the museum's collections.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (38)/2.png. Identification of subjects remains unknown.

title: Smith Robertson class portrait with young children
description: Group portrait of schoolchildren posed outside a school building.
significance: Documents student life and school culture connected to Black education history in Jackson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (38)/3.png. School attribution is provisional pending object file confirmation.

title: Smith Robertson class portrait with teacher
description: Large group class portrait with students and a teacher posed outside a school building.
significance: Offers visual documentation of school community and classroom life connected to Black public education.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (38)/4.png. Some image wear visible around edges.

title: Piney Woods pennant ('21)
description: Purple and gold pennant reading '21 Piney Woods.'
significance: Connects the collection to Black educational institutions and school identity in Mississippi.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (38)/5.png. Exact institution/date meaning should be confirmed.

title: Inventory of Jerry and Sue Whitt donation
description: Donation inventory sheet listing objects given permanently to the Smith Robertson Museum and Cultural Center by Jerry and Sue Whitt.
significance: Documents provenance and donor history for a group of objects entering the museum collection in 2007.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/1.png. Listed objects include Village Life, Child Taken by Mermaid, Zimbabwe woven hat, and framed Zimbabwe paintings.

title: Mounted Smith Robertson commencement program and tuition receipt
description: Mounted display containing a Smith Robertson School commencement program and an earlier school tuition receipt.
significance: Brings together rare documentary evidence of school life, ceremony, and payment records tied to the early history of Smith Robertson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/2.png. Tuition receipt handwriting is partially illegible and should be transcribed from the original object.

title: Carved wooden face mask
description: Tall carved wooden face mask with black incised design lines.
significance: Represents carved object holdings and may connect to African art, collecting, or exhibition history within the museum.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/3.png. Cultural attribution should be confirmed by collections staff.

title: Rules and Regulations for the Certification of the Administrative and Instructional Personnel (inscribed copy)
description: State Department of Education booklet outlining certification rules for administrative and instructional personnel, inscribed 'Betty Mae York.'
significance: Documents mid-century teacher certification policy and personal ownership by an educator connected to the collection.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/4.png.

title: Tomorrow Was Yesterday
description: Printed copy of the play 'Tomorrow Was Yesterday: A Play in Nine Scenes' by James N. Brown.
significance: Represents literary and dramatic materials that may support interpretation of Black intellectual and cultural history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/5.png.

title: On-the-Job Education in Rural Communities (inscribed title-page copy)
people: Roy W. Roberts; Betty Mae York
description: Title-page view of 'On-the-Job Education in Rural Communities,' edited by Roy W. Roberts, with ownership inscription 'Betty Mae York.'
significance: Documents educational thought and rural community instruction in the postwar period.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/6.png. May be the same physical publication as SRM-2026-086 photographed from a different side.

title: On-the-Job Education in Rural Communities (cover copy)
description: Cover view of the yearbook 'On-the-Job Education in Rural Communities.'
significance: Adds cover-design evidence for an educational publication tied to rural instruction and teacher training.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/7.png. May represent the same physical publication as SRM-2026-085.

title: Closing Day Treasury
description: Printed booklet titled 'Closing Day Treasury.'
significance: May have been used in school or youth programming, offering insight into recitation, ceremony, or classroom culture.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/8.png.

title: Recitation Time
description: Printed booklet titled 'Recitation Time: A Collection of Bright Recitations.'
significance: Reflects historical classroom materials and school performance culture for primary and intermediate grades.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/9.png.

title: School Laws of the State of Mississippi
description: Issued state school law booklet for Mississippi education administration.
significance: Provides legal context for how schools were governed in Mississippi during segregation-era education history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/10.png.

title: Fortieth Annual Convention of the Fifth Educational District of Mississippi Teachers Association
description: Convention program for the Fifth Educational District of the Mississippi Teachers Association at Carver Jr. High School.
significance: Documents teacher professional networks and Black education leadership in Mississippi in the 1960s.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/11.png.

title: Rules and Regulations for the Certification of the Administrative and Instructional Personnel (plain copy)
description: Additional copy of a State Department of Education certification booklet for instructional and administrative personnel.
significance: Useful for comparing editions, ownership marks, or circulation history within the collection.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/12.png. May duplicate or accompany SRM-2026-083.

title: The County Superintendent of Schools in the United States
description: Yearbook publication titled 'The County Superintendent of Schools in the United States.'
significance: Represents professional educational literature that may have informed school administration and teacher practice.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/13.png.

title: Souvenir Program of Miss Tan America Morton Pageant
description: Souvenir program for the 1965 Miss Tan America Morton Pageant at Bettye Mae Jack High School.
significance: Documents Black beauty culture, student performance, and community pageantry in mid-century Mississippi.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/14.png.

title: The Southern Register (Fall 1985)
description: Issue of The Southern Register, newsletter of the Center for the Study of Southern Culture at the University of Mississippi.
significance: Includes coverage of southern culture scholarship and a Richard Wright symposium, offering context for literary and cultural interpretation.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/15.png.

title: From Nancy Cabot's Scrapbook quilt clipping
description: Newspaper clipping featuring quilt block patterns under the heading 'From Nancy Cabot's Scrapbook.'
significance: Useful for documenting domestic arts, print culture, and quilting references within educational or community history collections.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/16.png.

title: Bound scrapbook or notebook with inserted typed historical document
format_material: bound volume with inserted typed pages
description: Composite image showing a worn bound volume with handwritten content and an inserted typed historical document.
significance: May contain research, personal notes, or documentary material that warrants closer archival processing.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/17.png. Detailed identification should be completed from the original object before public release.

title: Beaded gourd vessel with white and red netting
description: Rounded gourd vessel covered in netted beadwork with red and white beads.
significance: Represents object holdings that may connect to African or diasporic craft traditions and collecting history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/18.png. Cultural attribution should be confirmed.

title: Beaded gourd vessel marked Kuba
description: Tapered gourd vessel with multicolored bead netting and the inscription 'Kuba.'
significance: May indicate a collected object associated with Kuba art or a museum interpretation of African cultural material.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/19.png. Attribution and meaning of inscription should be verified.

title: A Neighborhood Discovery exhibition brochure
description: Printed exhibition brochure titled 'A Neighborhood Discovery' featuring a historic meeting image and Smith Robertson Senior Citizen Center information.
significance: Connects public history interpretation, neighborhood storytelling, and exhibition programming tied to Jackson's Black history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/20.png.

title: Black Fashion Museum exhibition brochure
description: Brochure for the Black Fashion Museum exhibition 'Salute to Black Designers: Past and Present.'
significance: Documents Black fashion history and exhibition culture, expanding the museum's interpretive connections beyond Jackson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/21.png.

title: Farish Street Historic District brochure
description: Printed brochure titled 'Farish Street Historic District' featuring Smith Robertson School and humanities project information.
significance: Supports interpretation of Farish Street and the historic importance of Smith Robertson School within Jackson's Black history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/22.png.

title: Opera/South program packet
description: Multi-piece Opera/South packet including program materials and related printed documents.
significance: Documents regional performing arts programming and cultural partnerships connected to the museum's broader historical context.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/23.png. May contain multiple related pieces that could later be cataloged separately.

title: SRA Reading Laboratory IIa student record book
description: SRA Reading Laboratory IIa student record book with handwritten entries and interior pages shown.
significance: Offers rare evidence of classroom practice, reading instruction, and student use within the school setting.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/24.png. Student name appears to read James Earl Dixon, but should be verified from the object.

title: SRA Reading Laboratory IIc student record book
description: SRA Reading Laboratory IIc student record book with heavily worn cover and interior record page.
significance: Useful for studying literacy instruction and the material condition of classroom tools used by students.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/25.png. Student name on the cover should be confirmed directly from the original.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Eleanor Curtis Citis? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Documents student enrollment, address, guardianship, and school assignment during the segregation era.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/26.png. Student name transcription is provisional and should be verified from the original document.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Harry Patty Ann? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Part of a surviving group of student admission forms documenting family addresses and educational access in 1957 Jackson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/27.png. Student name and exact spelling are provisional.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Patricia Andrea Jones? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Helps document individual students, households, and school enrollment patterns in segregated Jackson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/28.png. Student name transcription is provisional.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Richard Oddie? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Supports family and student-level research into the school's enrollment history.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/29.png. Student name transcription is provisional and should be verified.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Robin Lynn? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: One of a group of surviving admissions records documenting student assignment and family information.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/30.png. Student name transcription is provisional.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for James J. C. Horne
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for James J. C. Horne.
significance: Preserves named student enrollment documentation tied to Robertson School in 1957.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/31.png.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Lillie Mae White? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Adds to the set of surviving student assignment forms documenting Black educational history in Jackson.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/32.png. Student name appears to include Lillie Mae and White, but should be verified.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Carol Lula Jones
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for Carol Lula Jones.
significance: Named student file documenting enrollment at Robertson School during the 1957 term.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/33.png.

title: Jackson Public Schools admission application for Lindsey Sarah? (provisional)
description: Jackson Public Schools application for school admission and assignment for a Robertson School student.
significance: Extends the run of 1957 student admission files preserved in the collection.
notes: Source image: Untitled design (37)/34.png. Student name transcription is provisional and should be verified against the original document.
